response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.
response_parsed.opinion:
As an older man, I've seen many things in life, and now we're talking about the COVID vaccines. It's quite a topic because it affects everyone differently. My children are grown up now; one lives abroad studying medicine while my daughter is back home working as a teacher.

When discussing this with people on social media – which I do from time to time when they share articles or ask questions – I try not to get too technical since my education level is upper secondary and that's been years ago. But being curious helps me look into simple explanations online sometimes before joining the conversation.

I remember how important vaccines were during childhood, thanks even to my parents who had different backgrounds but both value health in their own ways. My dad got vocational diploma for cleaning skills back when there was no such thing as environmental science programs then – he always said keeping places clean prevents sicknesses.

My mom has a master's degree so she understands scientific principles better than me. We often discussed topics like this at home before I even knew what\'s best to do on social media or in person with friends now that we're older.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary:
1) Drawing from personal experience of aging and family background where children deal with healthcare discussions professionally.
2) Using limited time on social media but being open enough to engage when topics arise without getting overwhelmed by technical details.
3) Leveraging curiosity while respecting educational differences – my dad's practical approach versus mom's academic knowledge.
response_parsed.tone: Reflective and moderately curious, with a touch of agreeableness from past family discussions
